doc_code_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc_code_anatomy_content: Content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc_code_anatomy_root: Root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc_code_best_practice_1: Use Code consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc_code_best_practice_2: Test Code behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc_code_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Markdown better suits the use case.
doc_code_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_code_choice_self_when: When Code functionality with standard options is required.
doc_code_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_code_design_intent: Code provides a consistent, reusable pattern for displaying inline code or code blocks, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_code_long_desc: Used to display inline code or code blocks
doc_code_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_code_note_desc:
> Code blocks in the Code component (when the block property is true) have horizontal scrolling enabled.
> This is because forcibly wrapping long lines of code breaks indentation and structure, reducing readability.
doc_code_scenario_1: Integrating Code into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_code_scenario_2: Using Code in management views for information organization.
doc_code_title: Component for displaying inline code or code blocks

doc_errorboundary_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_errorboundary_choice_self_when: When ErrorBoundary functionality with standard options is required.
doc_errorboundary_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_errorboundary_custom_desc: By providing fallback as a function, you can freely customize the error message and reset button.
doc_errorboundary_def_desc: Displays a default error UI with a "Try again" button.
doc_errorboundary_design_intent: ErrorBoundary provides a consistent, reusable pattern for catching runtime errors and displaying fallback UI, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_errorboundary_feat_1_desc: Prevents the entire app from crashing due to the failure of a single component.
doc_errorboundary_feat_2_desc: You can provide your own fallback UI or use the default one.
doc_errorboundary_feat_3_desc: Includes a reset mechanism to retry rendering of child elements.
doc_errorboundary_long_desc: The ErrorBoundary component catches JavaScript errors that occur anywhere in its child component tree, logs those errors, and displays a fallback UI instead of the crashed component tree.
doc_errorboundary_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_errorboundary_scenario_1: Integrating ErrorBoundary into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_errorboundary_scenario_2: Using ErrorBoundary in management views for information organization.
doc_errorboundary_title: Boundary element to catch runtime errors and show fallback UI

doc_focustrap_anatomy_content: Content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc_focustrap_anatomy_root: Root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc_focustrap_auto_focus_desc: Setting the autoFocus prop to true shifts focus to the first focusable element as soon as the trap becomes active.
doc_focustrap_best_practice_1: Use FocusTrap consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc_focustrap_best_practice_2: Test FocusTrap behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc_focustrap_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Dialog better suits the use case.
doc_focustrap_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_focustrap_choice_self_when: When FocusTrap functionality with standard options is required.
doc_focustrap_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_focustrap_design_intent: FocusTrap provides a consistent, reusable pattern for trapping keyboard focus within a specific area for accessibility, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_focustrap_feat_1: Restricts Tab key movement within the component.
doc_focustrap_feat_2: Pressing Tab at the last element returns focus to the first, and pressing Shift + Tab at the first element moves it to the last (loop).
doc_focustrap_feat_3: When the component mounts (or becomes active), it can automatically shift focus to the first focusable element inside.
doc_focustrap_feat_4: When the component unmounts, it restores focus to the previously active element.
doc_focustrap_long_desc: FocusTrap is a utility component designed to trap keyboard focus within a specific area. It is used to implement accessible UIs such as modals, dialogs, and drawers where you want to restrict user interaction to a specific range.
doc_focustrap_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_focustrap_scenario_1: Integrating FocusTrap into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_focustrap_scenario_2: Using FocusTrap in management views for information organization.
doc_focustrap_title: Utility to keep keyboard focus within a specific area
doc_highlight_long_desc: The Highlight component emphasizes specific parts of a text using the mark tag.

doc_qrcode_desc_2: QRCode renders a specified string as a QR code. It supports output in SVG or Canvas format and allows customization of size, color, and error correction level.
doc_qrcode_design_intent: QRCode provides a consistent, reusable pattern for generating QR codes from text or URLs, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.

doc_qrcode_var_color_desc: Use bgColor and fgColor to change the background and foreground (dot) colors.
doc_qrcode_var_logo_desc: Use imageSettings to place a logo image in the center of the QR code.
doc_qrcode_var_size_desc: You can specify the width and height of the QR code with the size prop.
